text.title: OpenAI API Production Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Production rules for error handling, cost control, security, and agentic loop-specific failure modes.

rule.statement: Set `max_tokens` on every API call. Unbounded output generation runs up costs and stalls agents waiting for a response that never completes.
rule.rationale: Without max_tokens, a stuck generation can produce thousands of tokens. In agentic loops, this blocks all downstream steps. `finish_reason: "length"` signals truncation — check for it.

rule.statement: Implement exponential backoff for 429 (rate limit) and 5xx errors: start at 1s, cap at 60s, max 6 retries. Use `time.sleep(min(2**attempt, 60))` pattern.
rule.rationale: Rate limits are transient. Immediate retry hammers the quota further. Exponential backoff distributes retries over time and recovers from temporary limits gracefully.

rule.statement: Use service account keys (`sk-svcacct-*`) for automated systems — never user keys. Keep all API keys server-side; never expose them in client code.
rule.rationale: User keys have access to all projects in an account. Service account keys are scoped to a single project, limiting blast radius if compromised.

rule.statement: Enable prompt caching by placing static system prompts first and never modifying their prefix. Track cache hits via `response.usage` to verify caching is active.
rule.rationale: OpenAI's prompt cache activates on prefix matches. Even a single character change in a cached prefix invalidates it. Static system prompts placed first (before any dynamic content) maximize cache hit rate.

antipattern.description: Tool call response missing `tool_call_id` matching the request.
antipattern.reason: OpenAI requires each tool result to reference the exact `tool_call_id` from the request. Mismatches cause HTTP 400 errors. Always map results back to the call ID.

antipattern.description: Mixing Responses API and Chat Completions in the same conversation flow.
antipattern.reason: The two APIs manage conversation history differently. Responses API stores history server-side; Chat Completions requires developer-managed history. Mixing them produces inconsistent context.

antipattern.description: Submitting a Batch API job and then waiting synchronously for results.
antipattern.reason: Batch API results arrive asynchronously (up to 24h). Synchronous waiting will time out. Use a separate completion-handler agent or poll the batch status endpoint.

antipattern.description: Using `finish_reason: "length"` response downstream without checking truncation.
antipattern.reason: A truncated response means the model was cut off mid-output. Passing incomplete data downstream silently corrupts all dependent states. Always check finish_reason.
